items.title: E-commerce order table before modeling
items.scenario: A team loads order CSVs with duplicate `order_id` rows from a bad export, `price` stored as strings with `$` prefixes, and `country` values like `" US"` and `"US"`. They dedupe on `order_id`, strip and uppercase countries, coerce prices to float, then wrap imputer → one-hot → logistic regression in a ColumnTransformer inside one Pipeline fit on the training split.
items.explanation: pandas hygiene fixes schema-level issues; sklearn Pipeline ensures imputation and encoding statistics come from train only and replay identically at scoring time.

items.title: Healthcare features with mixed column types
items.scenario: A clinical risk model has numeric labs and categorical admission type. Numeric branch: median impute → StandardScaler. Categorical branch: most-frequent impute → OneHotEncoder(handle_unknown='ignore'). Both branches merge in ColumnTransformer, then feed a RandomForest—trees do not need scaling on numerics, but the team keeps the scaler for a future linear benchmark in the same pipeline template.
items.explanation: ColumnTransformer is the standard pattern when column subsets need different transform chains while sharing one fit/predict API.

items.title: Batch scoring API with serialized pipeline
items.scenario: After training, an engineer saves `joblib.dump(pipeline, 'churn_v3.joblib')`. The nightly batch job loads the artifact and calls `pipeline.predict_proba(new_rows)` without refitting medians on the batch. When retraining monthly, a new artifact version replaces the old file.
items.explanation: Persistence treats the fitted pipeline as the single source of truth for both transforms and model weights—production does not re-learn preprocessing from incoming data.

items.title: Cross-validation without leakage
items.scenario: A data scientist uses `cross_val_score(Pipeline([...]), X, y, cv=5)` instead of manually scaling the full X before CV. Each fold fits imputer and scaler on that fold's train slice only, then scores the fold's validation slice.
items.explanation: Bundling preprocessing inside Pipeline lets sklearn CV apply the same train-only discipline per fold automatically.
